text: DAY 50 AM ROUNDUP: Iran pushes back on Trump deal claims. Hormuz shut down again — Iran cites US 'piracy.' Enriched uranium sticking point — US says 'going very well,' Iran denies agreeing to handover. CENTCOM: 21 vessels turned back. Lebanon ceasefire violations continue. US sanctions 7 Iraqi militia commanders. Brent $97.68 (+22.3%).
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, NBC News, Tribune India, US Treasury confirmed.

text: Day 50 AM roundup: HORMUZ — Iran reverses opening, IRGC gunboats fire on tanker (no injuries). Ghalibaf: 'America's untrustworthiness.' TRUMP — 'good news' at Arizona rally, blockade 'in full force.' NUCLEAR — Iran rejects uranium handover. SANCTIONS — 7 Iraqi militia commanders designated. OIL — Brent $97.68 (+22.3%), WTI $93.74 (+24.1%). Gas $4.076. Waiver expires April 19. Ceasefire April 22 — 4 days.
verificationNote: Compiled from NBC News, CBS News, Irish Times, The National, Al-Monitor, Jerusalem Post, Tribune India, Bloomberg, US Treasury.

text: BREAKING: French UNIFIL peacekeeper SGM Florian Montorio killed in ambush at Ghandouriyeh, southern Lebanon. 3 more wounded. France blames Hezbollah. This is the second European military death in the Iran war. Macron demanding arrests. UNIFIL mission integrity at risk.
verificationNote: UN News, Al Jazeera, PBS News, Washington Times, Jerusalem Post confirmed.

text: DRAMATIC AUDIO from Strait of Hormuz: Indian tanker Sanmar Herald crew pleading after IRGC opened fire despite clearance — "You gave me clearance to go! You are firing now! Let me turn back!" 2 Indian ships forced to retreat. India summons Iranian ambassador. Massive diplomatic escalation.
verificationNote: NBC News, Newsweek, Tribune India, Onmanorama confirmed audio and diplomatic response.

text: Mojtaba Khamenei issues first Army Day (Farvardin 29) message — drones "strike like lightning" against US and Israel. Navy "ready for new bitter defeats." Also marks late father Ali Khamenei birthday. Increasingly belligerent tone despite ceasefire. Published via Telegram.
verificationNote: Iran International, ANI News, Free Press Journal, Al Jazeera confirmed.

text: SANCTIONS: 30-day Iran oil sanctions waiver officially EXPIRES today (April 19). Treasury Sec Bessent confirms NO renewal. ~140M barrels were covered. Russia waiver also expired. Full secondary sanctions snap back. China/India/Turkey most exposed. Oil at Brent $95.86 (+20.1%). Ceasefire expires April 22 — 3 days.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, Argus Media, GlobalSecurity confirmed waiver expiry and non-renewal.

text: Paris Hormuz Summit recap: 49 nations. Macron-Starmer co-chaired. UK-France-led multinational defensive naval mission announced for mine clearance and shipping escort. US NOT invited. 12+ countries offering naval assets. Military chiefs to meet at UK Northwood command next week. Iran calls it "provocative."
verificationNote: UK Gov joint statement, ABC News, Al Jazeera confirmed summit and naval mission.

text: Pakistan Army Chief Field Marshal Munir concludes 3-day Tehran visit — met Pezeshkian, Araghchi, Ghalibaf, military chief. Carrying new US proposals. Pushing for Round 2 Islamabad talks before April 22 ceasefire deadline. Sources say Iran "in principle agreed" to third-party nuclear monitoring.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Bloomberg confirmed Munir visit and push for Round 2.

text: Day 51 PM roundup: UNIFIL peacekeeper killed (France SGM Montorio). IRGC fires on Indian tankers — India summons ambassador. Khamenei Army Day: "strike like lightning." Sanctions waiver expired. Oil Brent $95.86 (+20.1%). Pakistan shuttle diplomacy in Tehran. Ceasefire April 22 — 3 days. Gas $4.076.
verificationNote: Compiled from UN News, NBC News, Iran International, Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, AAA.

text: TWO IDF SOLDIERS KIA IN LEBANON: Cmd. Sgt.-Maj. Barak Kalfon (48) died from pre-planted explosive wounds. Sgt. First Class Lidor Porat (31) killed in combat — 9 more wounded including 1 severely. Deadliest day since Lebanon ceasefire began. IDF says Kalfon device was pre-ceasefire — not a violation.
verificationNote: Jerusalem Post, Asharq Al-Awsat, Israel National News confirmed both fatalities.

text: CONTAINER SHIP HIT: Unidentified projectile struck container ship 25nm NE of Oman — UKMTO confirms. No injuries, damage to containers only, ship continued course. Comes hours after IRGC fired on Indian tankers. Gulf of Oman shipping increasingly dangerous.
verificationNote: Voice of Emirates, Caliber.Az, gCaptain confirmed incident and UKMTO report.

text: GHALIBAF REBUTTAL: Iran Parliament Speaker says Trump made 'seven claims in one hour, all seven false.' Specifically denies Iran agreed to return enriched uranium. Internal Iranian media also slams FM Araghchi's Hormuz tweet for letting Trump 'claim victory.' Hardliner-pragmatist split widening.
verificationNote: Iran International, Al Jazeera, Tribune India confirmed Ghalibaf rebuttal and Araghchi criticism.

text: DIPLOMACY UPDATE: SNSC confirms reviewing new US proposals via Pakistan mediation — no formal response yet. Round 2 contingent on US accepting 'battlefield realities.' EU Foreign Affairs Council Monday April 21 to discuss Israel sanctions and new Iran sanctions. Pope Leo XIV: 'not in my interest' to debate Trump.
verificationNote: Capital News Point, Defense News, NBC News, Euronews confirmed.

text: Day 51 Late roundup: TWO IDF KIA in Lebanon (Kalfon + Porat) — deadliest day since truce. Container ship hit off Oman. Trump Situation Room then golf. Ghalibaf: '7 lies in 1 hour.' Pope Leo won't debate Trump. SNSC reviewing US proposals. EU Council Monday. Markets closed — Brent $95.86 unchanged. Gas $4.076. Ceasefire April 22 — 3 days.
verificationNote: Compiled from Jerusalem Post, Voice of Emirates, Axios, NBC News, Capital News Point, AAA.

text: BREAKING: IDF confirms FIRST strikes on Hezbollah since Lebanon ceasefire. Struck tunnel shaft south of Yellow Line — found explosive devices, rockets, RPGs, grenades inside. Also struck operatives approaching IDF positions. Hezbollah MP contacted Iran about 'violations.' Trump had said Israel 'prohibited' from bombing, but US clarified defensive strikes allowed.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post confirmed IDF strikes on Hezbollah operatives.

text: DIPLOMACY STALL: Iran Deputy FM Khatibzadeh at Antalya Forum — 'no date set' for Round 2 until framework agreed. Calls uranium handover 'a nonstarter,' accuses US of 'maximalist' demands. Ghalibaf in separate televised address: 'far from final agreement.' Ceasefire expires April 22 — 3 days and counting.
verificationNote: PBS, Washington Times, RFE/RL, Euronews, CNBC confirmed.

text: SANCTIONS UPDATE: Iran oil sanctions waiver officially EXPIRED April 19 — US Treasury will NOT renew. Russian oil waiver RENEWED to May 16 (surprise reversal by Bessent under Asian pressure). Brent surges to $97.68 (+22.4%). US blockade tally: 23 vessels intercepted since April 13. HAYI (Iran-linked) arson attack on Jewish building in London — failed ignition, no injuries.
verificationNote: Moscow Times, Manila Times, RFE/RL, CNN confirmed.

text: CEASEFIRE FRAYING: IDF demolitions continue in Bint Jbeil. Kiryat Shmona municipality strikes — education shutdown, protest convoy to Jerusalem. Kallas accuses Russia of providing intelligence and drones to Iran. 49-nation Paris summit: UK-France-led defensive naval mission for Hormuz. EU Council Monday to discuss Israel + Iran sanctions.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, Euronews, RBC-Ukraine confirmed.

text: Day 51 AM roundup: IDF FIRST strikes on Hezbollah since ceasefire — tunnel destroyed. Khatibzadeh: 'no date for talks.' Ghalibaf: 'far from deal.' Trump: 'can't blackmail us.' Iran sanctions waiver EXPIRED. Russian waiver renewed May 16. HAYI arson London. Brent $97.68 (+22.4%). Gas $4.076. Ceasefire April 22 — 3 days.
verificationNote: Compiled from Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, RFE/RL, Euronews, PBS, Washington Times, CNN, Moscow Times, RFE/RL.

text: Trump Truth Social afternoon: 'NO MORE MR. NICE GUY!' — threatens to knock out every single power plant and bridge in Iran if deal rejected. Accuses Iran of 'total violation' of ceasefire. Sharpest public escalation since April 8 ceasefire. Ceasefire expires April 22 — 3 days.
verificationNote: TIME, Fortune, CBS News confirmed Truth Social post.

text: Pezeshkian afternoon address: 'Trump has no right to deny Iran nuclear rights.' Apologizes to neighbors, pledges they will not be targeted unless attacks originate from them — direct acknowledgment of March-April strikes on Qatar, Kuwait, Iraq. Positioning ahead of Monday Islamabad talks.
verificationNote: Capital News Point, Middle East Monitor, News on Air confirmed.

text: Strait of Hormuz Day 48 blockade snapshot: zero commercial passages past US blockade zone 24h running. IRGC gunboat posture aggressive following Apr 18 fire on Sanmar Herald and Jag Arnav container strike. Marine war-risk premiums still 10-60x baseline.
verificationNote: Lloyd's List, UKMTO, Al Jazeera confirmed vessel tracking and insurance data.

displayName: JINSA Iran Task Force — Policy Analyst
text: April Iran Task Force assessment ('End States, Not End Dates'): premature ceasefire without verified HEU removal risks Iranian nuclear breakout. Trump's Monday Islamabad delegation (Vance, Witkoff, Kushner) must anchor demands on end-state enrichment halt + HEU transfer.
verificationNote: JINSA April 2026 Iran Task Force publication.

text: Iran Update PM Day 51: Iranian banking sector now in crisis per ISW analyst channels. Officials describe economy as Iran's 'Achilles heel' as Iran oil sanctions waiver expires with no renewal. Shamkhani-linked shadow fleet (29 vessels) OFAC-designated April 15 continues to be degraded.
verificationNote: ISW/Critical Threats April 10 special report, OFAC April 15 designations, Moscow Times confirmed.

text: Netanyahu-Milei Jerusalem meeting formalizes 'Isaac Accords' — Israel's Latin America framework. Argentina is Israel's closest regional partner; framework designed to parallel 2020 Abraham Accords. Diplomatic hedge as US-Iran talks stall ahead of April 22 ceasefire expiry.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog April 19 confirmed.
